title: FreeBSD: nil pointer dereference in Container.NetworkMode() during inspect when network_mode=host via REST API
body:
- podman run --network host works correctly via CLI
- Same invocation via --url unix:///var/run/podman/podman.sock panics
- Container creation succeeds (HTTP 201), crash happens in the subsequent inspect call
- pf is loaded and active on the host
- Crash is in getContainerNetworkInfo → NetworkMode() called on nil *Container receiver
- Exact file: libpod/networking_common.go:225
- podman 5.7.1 on FreeBSD
- [log attached](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25979417/podman.log)

comments.body:
> Crash is in getContainerNetworkInfo → NetworkMode() called on nil *Container receiver

That seem wrong, your log is showing it failing on libpod/container.go:1435 which calls `ctrSpec.Linux.Namespaces` and then panic on freebsd because there is not Linux spec for a freebsd cotnainer

cc @dfr

Right, and the container I was trying to run had nothing to do with Linux. This was the Containerfile:

```
FROM ghcr.io/freebsd/freebsd-runtime:15.0

RUN pkg install -y fabio

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/sbin/fabio"]
```

For FreeBSD containers, it is normal that the Linux field in ctrSpec is nil. During the port, I added logic in quite a few places to not dereference ctrSpec.Linux if it was nil but either I missed this one or something was added which didn't have the extra check.

We are not running FreeBSD tests in CI which means these things turn up from time to time. Fixing that is quite a bit of work, getting changes to the test suite accepted and figuring out how to add a little FreeBSDI CI without slowing down any of the Linux work.
